Notice Of Defendants Motion For Relief Under C.C.P. Section 473
Off Calendar Aug-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand, Esq. (Hooshmand Law Group, 415.318.5709) for Plaintiff YARNG ALTAWAL;
Robert Carroll, III, Esq. (De La Pena & Holliday, 415.268.8000) for Defendant YOEL SBERLO, Individually and Trustee for the Sberlo Family Trust

The matter is argued and submitted. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS YOEL SBERLO, NADINE SBERLO and URBAN PIONEER, INC.'S Motion For Relief Under C.C.P. Section 473 is DENIED. Relief is not available. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that counsel's failure to properly notice the motion caused entry of judgement, dismissal or order, i.e. precludes the action from being addressed on the merits.

Plaintiff's counsel makes an oral motion for sanctions against defense counsel in the amount of $1,500 to address defendant's untimely filed motions. The Court is not moved by defense counsel's claims of errors pursuant to Civil Code of Procedure 473.

The Court orders plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit an order in comformity with the tentative ruling and leave a blank space for sanctions. Party to comply with CRC 3.1312(b).
Judge Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: L. Fong; Reporter: Amber C. Emerick, CSR #13546 (Emerick and Finch, T: 925.831.9029). (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Hooshmand (415-318-5709) of Hooshmand Law Group for Plaintiff/opposing party Yarng Altawal.
Anderson Franco (415-268-8000) of De La Pena & Holiday LLP for Defendants/Moving party YOEL SBERLO, NADINE SBERLO, URBAN PIONEER, INC.
Court reporter: Sarah Seitz CSR #14175 (619-233-2030).

The Court announces Tentative Ruling. ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs YOEL SBERLO, NADINE SBERLO, URBAN PIONEER, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant admits that there is a triable issue of fact as to the first cause of action for negligence. Therefore the Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendants failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) (Compare: (1) Notice of Motion "The doctrines also bar ALTAWAL's individual causes of action number two, three, four, and five;" and Separate Statement, e.g.: "Issue 1 - The first cause of action for negligence fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against defendants.") Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
